description_long: This object consists of six identical, boxy clock units arranged in a straight horizontal row, each resembling a classic analog camera with a prominent circular face on the front. The front of each unit features a round clock face set within a rectangular housing, with a raised section on top that mimics a camera's viewfinder or flash. The rear of each unit displays a rectangular panel, possibly a battery compartment or display window, highlighted in a contrasting color. The sides of the object reveal the depth and uniformity of each clock unit, with subtle detailing along the edges. The overall construction appears robust, with a metallic or hard plastic exterior and a matte finish. The design is utilitarian and modular, suggesting it could be used in public or industrial settings for synchronized timekeeping.
description: A row of six identical, boxy clocks with circular faces and raised top sections, resembling vintage cameras. The design is robust and modular, with contrasting panels on the back.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thin profile of the six connected clock units in a straight line.
description_view_1: The front view displays six identical clock faces, each set in a boxy housing with a raised top section.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlights the slim, uniform profile of the connected clocks.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals six rectangular panels, each with a contrasting color, likely for access or display.
attribute_face_design: Circular clock faces set within rectangular housings, each with a raised top section and minimalistic detailing.
